Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn check-no-config
  "The same as `check`, but ignores dotfile configuration."
  [{:keys [parallel? paths report] :as options
    :or   {report report/console}}])

Contract type: docstring_contract
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn check-no-config
  "The same as `check`, but ignores dotfile configuration."
  [{:keys [parallel? paths report] :as options
    :or   {report report/console}}]
  (let [context (report :check/initial options nil)
        map*    (if parallel? pmap map)
        summary (->> paths
                     (mapcat (partial find-files context))
                     (map* (partial check-one context))
                     (reduce #(report :check/file %1 %2) context))]
    (report :check/summary summary nil)))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn check
  "Checks that the Clojure paths specified by the :paths option are
  correctly formatted."
  [options])

Contract type: docstring_contract
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn check
  "Checks that the Clojure paths specified by the :paths option are
  correctly formatted."
  [options]
  (let [opts (merge (config/load-config) options)]
    (check-no-config opts)))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn fix-no-config
  "The same as `fix`, but ignores dotfile configuration."
  [{:keys [parallel? report] :as options
    :or   {report report/console}}])

Contract type: docstring_contract
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn fix-no-config
  "The same as `fix`, but ignores dotfile configuration."
  [{:keys [parallel? report] :as options
    :or   {report report/console}}]
  (let [context (report :fix/initial options nil)
        files   (recursively-find-files context)
        map*    (if parallel? pmap map)
        summary (->> files
                     (map* (partial fix-one context))
                     (reduce #(report :fix/file %1 %2) context))]
    (report :fix/summary summary nil)))

(defn fix
  "Fixes the formatting for all files specified by the :paths option."
  [options])

Contract type: docstring_contract

(defn fix
  "Fixes the formatting for all files specified by the :paths option."
  [options]
  (let [opts (merge (config/load-config) options)]
    (fix-no-config opts)))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn unalign-form
  "Remove any consecutive non-indenting whitespace within the form."
  [form])

Observed in repo tests:
- related test file: cljfmt/test/cljfmt/core_test.cljc

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn unalign-form
  "Remove any consecutive non-indenting whitespace within the form."
  [form]
  (-> form z/of-node z/down
      (edit-all non-indenting-whitespace? unalign-from-space z/right*)
      z/root))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn remove-blank-lines-in-forms
  [form blank-line-forms opts])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- (is (reformats-to? form form {:remove-blank-lines-in-forms? true})))))
- (is (reformats-to? form form {:remove-blank-lines-in-forms? true}))))

Contract type: test_contract
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn remove-blank-lines-in-forms [form blank-line-forms opts]
  (let [ns-name     (or (::ns-name opts) (find-namespace (z/of-node form)))
        context     {:alias-map (:alias-map opts)
                     :refer-map (:refer-map opts)
                     :ns-name ns-name}
        blank-line? #(blank-line-in-form? % blank-line-forms context)]
    (transform form edit-all blank-line? replace-with-single-newline)))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn wrap-normalize-newlines
  [f])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- (is (= ((wrap-normalize-newlines identity) "foo\nbar\nbaz") "foo\nbar\nbaz"))
- (is (= ((wrap-normalize-newlines identity) "foo\r\nbar\r\nbaz") "foo\r\nbar\r\nbaz"))

Contract type: test_contract
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn wrap-normalize-newlines [f]
  (fn [s]
    (let [sep (find-line-separator s)]
      (-> s normalize-newlines f (replace-newlines sep)))))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn read-config
  "Read a Clojure or edn configuration file."
  [file])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- (is (= {:key "value"} (config/read-config temp-file)))
- (is (= {:key "value"} (config/read-config temp-file)))

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn read-config
  "Read a Clojure or edn configuration file."
  [file]
  (let [contents (slurp file)]
    (case (filename-ext file)
      "clj" (read-string contents)
      "edn" (edn/read-string {:readers {'re re-pattern}} contents))))
